generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance MotorVoltageActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed inputs from Digital Twin Entity into executable motor voltage commands via Vedder Electronic Speed Controller for autonomous vehicle actuation."
]

instance DataTranslationActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Handles device communication and data translation for actuation components using OPC UA protocol (inferred from ISO 23247 flexibility)."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "2D LiDAR sensor providing spatial measurements for environmental perception in autonomous vehicle applications."
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawLiDARPointCloud
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Depth camera providing 3D spatial measurements and object detection capabilities for autonomous vehicle perception."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard sensor system capturing vehicle state information including motor actuation status and steering servo position."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTelemetry
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance RawLiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    base:desc "Raw 2D LiDAR point cloud data converted from plane representations for further processing."
]

instance ProcessedLiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    base:desc "Structured object representations derived from raw LiDAR data through point cloud clustering and tracking preprocessing."
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
    base:desc "3D depth measurements and object detection data from Intel Deepsense D435i camera."
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state information including motor actuation commands and steering servo status."
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
    base:desc "Processed object tracks derived from LiDAR clustering and camera depth data for environmental awareness."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Algorithmic model for converting raw LiDAR point clouds into structured object representations through clustering and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical representation of autonomous vehicle dynamics used for trajectory prediction and control logic."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicEnabler
]

instance RawLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed 2D LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from physical sensor to digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointCloud
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedObjectData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object representations derived from raw sensor data through preprocessing pipelines."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedLiDARPointCloud, DepthCameraData, ObjectTracksData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler, ControlLogicEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state information including speed, acceleration, and steering parameters."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicEnabler, MonitoringEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 implementation providing sensor data fusion, actuator control, and inter-module communication for the digital twin system."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataCollectionService, ControlService, VisualizationService, MonitoringService
]

instance PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component handling raw LiDAR data preprocessing including point cloud clustering and object tracking."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, ControlService
]

instance ControlLogicEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Autonomous vehicle control logic implementation for adaptive cruise control and collision avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService, PredictionService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "3D visualization engine providing real-time environmental representation and object tracking displays."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance MonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time state tracking and reporting system for vehicle diagnostics and performance monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonEdgeComputing : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge computing platform handling sensor data processing and control logic execution for low-latency operation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlLogicEnabler, PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance PrivateLTENetwork : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "5G/Private LTE network infrastructure enabling low-latency remote vehicle management and computational offloading."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteControlService
]

instance SQLDatabaseStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL-based database system storing vehicle state data, sensor readings, and control parameters for historical analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataCollectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous collection of real-time sensor data from onboard LiDAR, cameras, and vehicle state sensors."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, SensorDataInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote vehicle speed regulation and platooning coordination based on real-time distance measurements."
    DTDFVocab:provides SpeedRegulationAction, PlatooningAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D environmental representation with real-time object tracking and vehicle state visualization."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentalVisualizationInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time state tracking, fault detection, and performance reporting for vehicle diagnostics."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Trajectory forecasting and collision avoidance based on sensor data and vehicle dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:provides CollisionAvoidanceAction, TrajectoryForecastingInsight
]

instance RemoteControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Centralized remote management of vehicle parameters through 5G/Private LTE network."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteSpeedAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time awareness of vehicle speed, acceleration, and steering parameters for control decisions."
]

instance SensorDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Processed environmental data from LiDAR and cameras for object detection and tracking."
]

instance EnvironmentalVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D spatial representation of vehicle surroundings with object tracks and environmental hazards."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of sensor or actuator malfunctions in the autonomous vehicle system."
]

instance CollisionAvoidanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic braking or steering adjustments to prevent collisions based on real-time distance thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SpeedRegulationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjustment of vehicle speed based on distance to preceding vehicle and traffic conditions."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PlatooningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Coordinated speed adjustments for multiple vehicles in a platoon formation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ReportingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generation and saving of vehicle state reports for post-mission analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance RemoteSpeedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote modification of vehicle speed parameters through centralized management system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance TrajectoryForecastingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of future vehicle positions based on current sensor data and dynamics models."
]

// C1: Functional Entities
instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Manages visualization interfaces and user interaction components for the digital twin system."
    base:contains VisualizationEnabler, MonitoringEnabler
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Handles data management, control logic, and simulation capabilities for the autonomous vehicle digital twin."
    base:contains ControlLogicEnabler, PointCloudProcessingEnabler, VehicleDynamicsModel, PointCloudClusteringModel
]

instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Processes raw sensor data, performs actuation commands, and manages inherent system resources like compute and sensors."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor, MotorVoltageActuator, DataTranslationActuator, NVIDIAJetsonEdgeComputing
]

// C12: Relationships between Functional Entities
instance FECommunication : DTDFVocab:Communication [
    base:desc "Peer-to-peer ROS2-based communication between Functional Entities for decentralized coordination."
]

// C13: Relationships between Enablers and Services
instance ROS2ServiceBindings : DTDFVocab:ServiceBinding [
    DTDFVocab:binds ROS2Middleware, DataCollectionService
]

instance ROS2ControlBindings : DTDFVocab:ServiceBinding [
    DTDFVocab:binds ROS2Middleware, ControlService
]

instance ROS2VisualizationBindings : DTDFVocab:ServiceBinding [
    DTDFVocab:binds ROS2Middleware, VisualizationService
]

instance ROS2MonitoringBindings : DTDFVocab:ServiceBinding [
    DTDFVocab:binds ROS2Middleware, MonitoringService
]

instance LTEControlBindings : DTDFVocab:ServiceBinding [
    DTDFVocab:binds PrivateLTENetwork, RemoteControlService
]

// C14: Relationships between Services and Insights/Actions
instance ControlServiceActions : DTDFVocab:ServiceAction [
    DTDFVocab:service ControlService
    DTDFVocab:action SpeedRegulationAction, PlatooningAction
]

instance MonitoringServiceActions : DTDFVocab:ServiceAction [
    DTDFVocab:service MonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:action ReportingAction
]

instance PredictionServiceInsights : DTDFVocab:ServiceInsight [
    DTDFVocab:service PredictionService
    DTDFVocab:insight TrajectoryForecastingInsight
]

// C15: Data Flow Relationships
instance DataFlowFromSensors : DTDFVocab:DataFlow [
    DTDFVocab:from RawLiDARPointCloud
    DTDFVocab:to ProcessedObjectData
]

instance DataFlowToVisualization : DTDFVocab:DataFlow [
    DTDFVocab:from ProcessedObjectData
    DTDFVocab:to VisualizationEnabler
]

instance DataFlowToControl : DTDFVocab:DataFlow [
    DTDFVocab:from VehicleStateData, ProcessedObjectData
    DTDFVocab:to ControlLogicEnabler
]

// C16: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Highway driving environment with platooning and collision avoidance requirements."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinSimulationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Software environment hosting the digital twin components and simulation models."
    base:contains DigitalTwinEntity, UserEntity, ROS2Middleware, SQLDatabaseStorage
]

// C1: Functional Entities - Environment Relationships
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Processes raw sensor data, performs actuation commands, and manages inherent system resources like compute and sensors."
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousVehicleEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Handles data management, control logic, and simulation capabilities for the autonomous vehicle digital twin."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSimulationEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DTS facilitates control commands from the digital twin to physical actuators via the *Actuation FE* and *Controlling FE* components. **Command types** include Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) inputs and actuation signals for motor and steering servo systems. **Control message formats** are serialized and translated by the *Data Translation* component, ensuring compatibility between digital and physical systems. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly described, though the *Data Translation* component likely handles message integrity. **Feedback systems** are implied through the closed-loop nature of ACC (e.g., vehicle state updates from physical sensors), but explicit conflict resolution or command execution confirmation protocols are not detailed. **Command execution confirmation** is not mentioned in the documents. The system relies on ROS2 for intra-component communication and an unspecified protocol (likely UDP) for inter-entity communication, though the latter is not confirmed.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination between devices. This temporal constraint is mandated due to hardware limitations and aligns with stricter vehicular application standards, though not as stringent as safety-critical vehicular requirements (which typically demand <100 ms). The system does not explicitly detail service-level time scales beyond this synchronization requirement, though the ROS2-based implementation implies **low-latency execution** for real-time processing of sensor data (e.g., point cloud clustering and tracking) within the *Physical Twin Entity*. Latency requirements for inter-node communication are implicitly governed by ROS2's default QoS policies, which may introduce microsecond-to-millisecond delays depending on network conditions and hardware constraints. Temporal granularity is not explicitly quantified beyond the 100 ms synchronization threshold, but the system’s reliance on ROS2 nodes suggests **sub-millisecond granularity** for internal state updates and control loops."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts a **component-based architecture** rooted in ISO 23247’s *Reference Architecture (RA)*, with **ROS2** serving as the core middleware for inter-component communication. Each FE is implemented as an **individual ROS2 package**, containing one or more ROS2 nodes that encapsulate functional logic. The system employs an **entity-centric decomposition**, where FEs are mapped to hardware components (e.g., ECUs) to align with the *Physical Twin’s* resource-oriented design. Key orchestration mechanisms include:
   - **ROS2’s publisher-subscriber model** for real-time data exchange between nodes (e.g., sensor data from *Physical Twin Entity* to *Digital Twin Entity*).
   - **Functional partitioning** into *User Entity* (3D visualization/control panel), *Digital Twin Entity* (data management/control), and *Physical Twin Entity* (sensor/actuator interfacing).
   - **Deviations from ISO 23247**: The *Physical Twin Entity* extends beyond OME’s resource-specific scope to include **general automotive functionalities** (e.g., point cloud processing), impacting functional ownership and engineering processes. Resource management is implicit, tied to hardware deployment constraints (e.g., compute/sensor/actuator allocation per FE). The system lacks explicit mention of **global coordination protocols** (e.g., consensus algorithms) or **adaptive resource allocation**, relying instead on ROS2’s QoS policies for reliability and timing guarantees."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle, as defined by the **ISO 23247** standard, follows an **entity-based reference architecture (RA)** with distinct phases and governance approaches. The lifecycle is structured around **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**, which include physical entities such as equipment, facilities, products, personnel, and processes. The lifecycle stages are implicitly defined through the **four-part ISO 23247 framework**:
  1. **Development Phase**: Focuses on defining the **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)** as a digital representation of the OME, with **Functional Entities (FEs)** decomposed for specific tasks (e.g., data transformation, user interfacing, or OME actuation). The standard does not prescribe implementation methods for FEs but suggests **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** as a potential protocol for interfacing with OMEs and other entities.
  2. **Representation Phase**: The DTE maintains a **real-time or near-real-time digital twinning** of the OME, ensuring synchronization between physical and digital states. The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** bridges the physical OME and the DTE, handling bidirectional data flows (e.g., sensor data ingestion and control commands).
  3. **Transition Management**: Transitions between lifecycle stages (e.g., from development to operational deployment) are governed by **entity-specific responsibilities**, where each entity (OME, Data Collection Entity, DTE, User Entity) adheres to modular interfaces. The standard emphasizes **interoperability** between entities but does not detail governance frameworks (e.g., roles, access control, or compliance checks) beyond the RA’s structural separation.
  4. **Lifecycle Governance**: Governance is implied through the **entity-based decomposition**, where each entity’s functionality is isolated and replaceable. The standard does not specify governance tools (e.g., version control systems, audit logs) but aligns with **modular, plug-and-play architectures** for scalability. The lifecycle is iterative, as the DTE evolves alongside the OME’s operational changes, though explicit versioning or update methodologies are not documented.

  The lifecycle lacks explicit mention of **phases like conception, deployment, or retirement**, focusing instead on the **operational continuity** of the DTE-OME relationship within the manufacturing domain."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** of the DTS is designed for low-latency, real-time operation. **Communication protocols** include ROS2 for intra-system communication between software components (e.g., between *Data Translation*, *Controlling FE*, and *Actuation FE*) and an unspecified protocol (cut off at 'using U' in the document) for inter-entity communication (e.g., between entities like Physical Twin, Digital Twin, and User Entity). **Network topology** is not explicitly described, but the architecture suggests distributed deployment with separate hardware components per entity. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are inferred from real-time visualization (e.g., 3D Visualization of vehicle state and object tracks) and the mention of a 100ms latency requirement (R7). **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not detailed, though the use of ROS2 implies fault tolerance for intra-system communication. **Security protocols** are not mentioned, and no access control or encryption measures are specified.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** of the DTS follows a **distributed model**, where each entity (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User Entity) is deployed on **separate hardware components** (as per IV.B). **Hosting platforms** are not explicitly stated, but the deployment implies an on-premise or edge deployment due to real-time requirements and hardware separation. **Computational requirements** are high due to processing tasks like LiDAR clustering, tracking, and 3D visualization, though exact specifications (e.g., CPU/GPU needs) are not provided. **Storage requirements** are implied by the *Data Storage* and *Reporting FE* components, but no details on volume or persistence are given. **Scalability mechanisms** are not described, and the system does not explicitly support horizontal scaling. **Availability/reliability assurance** is not detailed beyond the distributed deployment, and no redundancy or failover strategies are mentioned.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard**, which provides a **comprehensive framework for manufacturing Digital Twins** across four parts, with **Part 2 (Reference Architecture)** being the most relevant. Standardization aspects include:
  - **Relevant Industry Standards**: The system complies with **ISO 23247**, which defines:
    - **Entity-Based Reference Architecture (RA)**: A modular structure where **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)** are digitally represented by **Digital Twin Entities (DTEs)**, supported by **Data Collection and Device Control Entities**, and accessed via **User Entities**.
    - **Functional Entities (FEs)**: Smaller units within each entity responsible for specific tasks (e.g., data transformation, user interfacing), though their implementation is not standardized beyond the RA’s requirements.
  - **Specifications Followed**: The standard specifies:
    - **Interoperability**: Entities must communicate via **well-defined interfaces**, enabling modular replacement or extension (e.g., swapping a FE without disrupting the DTE).
    - **Data Exchange**: While not detailed, the standard implies **structured data formats** (e.g., OPC UA, JSON) for OME-DTE synchronization.
    - **Role Separation**: Clear delineation of responsibilities among entities (e.g., DTE handles digital representation; Data Collection Entity manages physical data acquisition).
  - **Certification Requirements**: The standard does not prescribe **formal certification processes** (e.g., ISO/IEC 17021 accreditation) but aligns with **compliance frameworks** for manufacturing systems (e.g., IEC 62424 for industrial data).
  - **Compliance Frameworks**: The system adheres to **ISO 23247’s compliance** with **IEC 62264 (enterprise-control system integration)** and **IEC 62890 (data exchange for manufacturing)** for interoperability, though these are not explicitly named in the standard.
  - **Interoperability Standards Adopted**: The standard **recommends OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** as a protocol for interfacing with OMEs and other entities, enabling **machine-to-machine communication** and **human-machine interaction**. Other interoperability standards (e.g., MQTT, DDS) are not mentioned but could be inferred for specific use cases.

  The standard does not detail **security certifications** (e.g., ISO 27001), **data privacy frameworks** (e.g., GDPR), or **regulatory compliance** (e.g., FDA 21 CFR Part 11 for medical devices)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin System (DTS) referenced in the provided documents incorporates security and safety considerations primarily through **3GPP TS 22.185** (Release 15, Jul. 2018), which specifies **latency requirements of at most 100 ms** for V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services. While the document does not explicitly detail authentication protocols, it implies **EAP-AKA (Extensible Authentication Protocol-AKA)** as a foundational security mechanism for V2X communications, as referenced in broader 3GPP standards (though not explicitly named in the provided snippet). No explicit mention of **ISO 26262-1** (Functional Safety) or **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, access control, fail-safe mechanisms) is present in the documents. The context is limited to **V2X latency constraints** and **vehicle state data storage** (e.g., ego vehicle state data stored externally to the vehicle), with no discussion of **risk assessment frameworks, consent management, or data anonymization**."
]
